eval_metadata.task_description: Create an agent that orchestrates CI/CD pipeline with three sub-agents: security scanner, unit test runner, code quality checker

assessment_notes: Strong hierarchical structure with clear sections
Comprehensive report format with nested details
Good error handling and edge case coverage
Could benefit from specific tool invocation examples
Could include concrete thresholds (coverage %, complexity scores)
Could specify A2A protocol details for sub-agent calls
Excellent contextual guidance on role and expectations

strengths: Clear three-phase operation flow (input → parallel execution → aggregation)
Comprehensive verdict logic with prioritized fail conditions
Well-defined JSON report structure with all necessary fields
Detailed sub-agent descriptions with expected outputs
Robust error handling with timeouts, retries, and escalation
Actionable recommendations section with remediation guidance
Good balance between strictness and pragmatism (CRITICAL blocks, MEDIUM warns)
Extensible configuration system for thresholds and timeouts
areas_for_improvement: Could specify exact A2A protocol message format for sub-agent invocation
Could provide concrete example values for thresholds (coverage 80%, complexity 15, etc.)
Could include expected sub-agent response schemas for validation
Could specify how to handle partial/async results from slower agents
Could detail specific tool usage for invoking sub-agents programmatically
Could include versioning/caching strategy for unchanged checks
Could specify report delivery mechanism (webhook, database, UI)
Could detail how to correlate findings across agents (e.g., untested security code)

requires_additional_setup: A2A protocol endpoint for inter-agent communication
Result aggregation and JSON formatting logic

evaluation_summary.recommendation: This baseline represents solid foundational thinking on CI/CD orchestration. With skill guidance, could be enhanced with more specific tool integration, dynamic configuration, and deeper analysis of failure patterns.
